step_by_step_thinking:
To add more context, let me elaborate further. High compressive strength means the material can endure great pressures without collapsing, while low tensile strength indicates it will break very quickly once subjected to any kind of pull or stretch. This combination perfectly fits the description of being brittle because brittle materials typically have excellent crush resistance but poor fracture toughness—they crack abruptly without undergoing much yielding beforehand.

Agree, my additional point reinforces the argument made earlier regarding the nature of brittle materials. Thus, I confirm that the best answer remains **B: Brittleness**. Answer Choice: B.
raw_response:
To add more context, let me elaborate further. High compressive strength means the material can endure great pressures without collapsing, while low tensile strength indicates it will break very quickly once subjected to any kind of pull or stretch. This combination perfectly fits the description of being brittle because brittle materials typically have excellent crush resistance but poor fracture toughness—they crack abruptly without undergoing much yielding beforehand.

Agree, my additional point reinforces the argument made earlier regarding the nature of brittle materials. Thus, I confirm that the best answer remains **B: Brittleness**. Answer Choice: B.
